what_was_done: Implemented BASIC DATA, READ and RESTORE support.
Added DATA runtime state and scanner in basic_data.s8.
Added DATA/READ/RESTORE keywords and OUT OF DATA error string.
Added READ and RESTORE command handlers and extracted them into basic_data_cmd.s8.
Fixed destructive-CMP stack corruption in GOSUB/RETURN/FOR/NEXT in basic_flow.s8.
Prevented BASIC POKE 38400/38401 from overwriting interpreter code by moving the IF-and-later basic_stmt code past 0x9600 with an .org gap and relocating DATA command handlers out of the hot region.
Updated the BASIC auto test expectations for DATA/READ/RESTORE and filtered the trailing REPL prompt in the CMake integration test.
what_was_found: Sophia8 CMP is destructive. Using CMP directly on stack pointers corrupted GOSUB/FOR bookkeeping and overwrote memory.
User POKE at decimal 38400/38401 writes to 0x9600/0x9601. After the DATA implementation grew the binary, those addresses landed inside interpreter code, which broke later string READ/IF execution.
The remaining full-test failure was therefore not a DATA parser bug but a memory-map/code-placement regression caused by code growth.
next_to_do: Consider reserving the 0x9600 screen/MMIO region explicitly in the machine/context documentation so future code growth cannot silently overlap it.
Consider adding a dedicated regression test that verifies POKE 38400/38401 does not break later IF/string/DATA execution.
Consider moving more BASIC command handlers into separate modules to keep the core layout stable and reduce future overlap risk.
